question: What do Figures 1.2-2 and 1.2-3 show about the NuScale functional boundaries and the single NPM with secondary equipment?

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It explains the types of interface requirements (conceptual design information and COL information items), and describes how COL applicants must address site-specific information and any departures from the certified design. Tables referenced in the section summarize these interfaces and list the required COL information items.

nuscale_ch01_0017.title: Facility Description
nuscale_ch01_0017.summary: The Facility Description section outlines the NuScale plant’s core and cooling system design. It explains the natural circulation process within the reactor, the generation of superheated steam to drive a turbine and produce electricity, and the role of the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) in supporting auxiliary systems and heat removal. Equipment layout and operation details are site-specific, with further information provided in referenced sections.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the radioactive waste management system at the NuScale Power Plant, noting that liquid, gaseous, and solid waste systems are detailed in Chapter 11, along with effluent monitoring. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), which is a reinforced, seismically designed concrete structure housing the nuclear power modules and providing radiation protection, with specific layout and safety features.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It describes how certain structures, systems, and components are divided between the certified and site-specific designs, and outlines the types of interface requirements (conceptual design information and COL information items) that must be addressed by the COL applicant, including the need to identify any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references figures for detailed layouts and notes that some buildings, like the administration and warehouse, are outside the report’s scope.

nuscale_ch01_0016.title: Principal Site Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0016.summary: This section provides a general overview of the NuScale plant design, outlining key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and supplement it with site-specific information, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI). Security-related details are specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0511.title: DSRS 11.6, Rev 0: Guidance on Instrumentation and Control Design Features for Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring, and Area Radiation and Airborne Radioactivity MonitoringII.1 Installation of instrumentation or sampling equipment
nuscale_ch01_0511.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the installation of instrumentation and sampling equipment related to radiological monitoring. It indicates that the design features and monitoring locations generally conform to DSRS 11.6 requirements, with some site-specific operational program responsibilities deferred to the COL applicant. Partial conformance is noted for certain post-TMI action plan items, specifically those related to testing and operational programs.

nuscale_ch01_0797.title: SRP 15.7.5, Rev 2: Spent Fuel Cask Drop AccidentsII.2 Radioactivity Control Features of Fuel Storage and Handling Systems
nuscale_ch01_0797.summary: The NuScale reactor building crane (RBC) is designed to be single-failure-proof per NUREG-0612, ensuring that no credible single component failure can result in dropping a critical load such as a spent fuel cask. Because of this robust design, load drop evaluations and accident analyses for radiological consequences are not required or performed for spent fuel cask or NPM drop accidents. Thus, the section concludes that the radioactivity control features for fuel storage and handling systems are not applicable in this context.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be supplied by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols and diagrams used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0510.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.7 Descriptions of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0510.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological monitoring and sampling systems, specifically addressing the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It notes that NuScale’s design generally conforms to the relevant DSRS and NUREG-0800 requirements, with some site-specific operational program responsibilities deferred to the COL applicant. Partial conformance is noted for certain post-TMI action plan items, where testing and operational programs are also the COL applicant’s responsibility.

nuscale_ch01_0796.title: SRP 15.7.5, Rev 2: Spent Fuel Cask Drop AccidentsII.1 Acceptability of Site and Dose Mitigating ESF Systems
nuscale_ch01_0796.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the reactor building crane (RBC) is designed to be single-failure-proof per NUREG-0612, ensuring that any credible single component failure will not result in dropping a critical load. Because of this robust design, load drop evaluations and accident analyses for spent fuel cask or NPM drops, including assessments of radiological consequences and the need for dose mitigating engineered safety features (ESF), are not required or applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference and supplement the NuScale FSAR with site-specific details. Security-related information is specially marked and withheld according to regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple modules using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for plant operations, with additional display stations for shared systems and diagnostics.
